evals.prompt: How should I suppress a TypeScript error I know is safe?
evals.expected_output: Use @ts-expect-error (not @ts-ignore) with a comment explaining why. @ts-expect-error is self-documenting and fails at build time if the error later disappears, preventing stale suppressions. @ts-ignore silently ignores the error forever with no feedback when it's no longer needed.

evals.prompt: My TypeScript bundler builds fine but I have hidden type errors. What am I missing?
evals.expected_output: Bundlers like esbuild, tsup, and Vite skip type-checking — they only transpile. Always run tsc --noEmit in CI as a separate step to catch type errors. Add a type-check script to package.json and include it in your CI pipeline before the build step.

evals.prompt: How do I set up pre-commit linting for a TypeScript project?
evals.expected_output: Use lint-staged with Husky. Add lint-staged config to package.json mapping *.ts/*.tsx to eslint --fix and prettier --write. Create .husky/pre-commit to run npx lint-staged && npm run type-check. Install with: npx husky init. This ensures only staged files are linted rather than the whole project.

should_not_trigger: Write a Vitest test for an async TypeScript service that throws NotFoundError
